name: Engaña Tunnel and Yera Station Loop
name_original: Túnel de la Engaña por el arroyo Aján – circular desde Vega de Pas
description: Explore the Túnel de la Engaña por el arroyo Aján circular mountain biking route from Vega de Pas, featuring waterfalls and history.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Vega De Pas offers diverse landscapes within the Valles Pasiegos region of Cantabria, Spain. The area features green meadows, dense forests, and a distinctive karst and limestone morphology, shaped by glacial origins. River valleys like the Pas, Pisueña, and Miera carve through the terrain, while mountain passes such as Alto del Caracol and Puerto de Lunada provide significant elevation changes. This varied environment creates a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Vega De Pas The most popular mountain bike trail is Engaña Tunnel and Yera Station Loop, a 13.1 miles (21.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic Cantabrian Cordillera, passing by the historic Engaña Tunnel. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Alto Alcor and Rubienzo Stream Loop, a difficult 13.9 miles (22.3 km) path. This trail features challenging gradients and offers views of the region's natural features. Local mountain bikers also love the Cascadas del río Troja – circular desde San Pedro del Romeral por el alto de la Matanela, a 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail leading through river valleys and past waterfalls, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Vega De Pas is defined by green meadows, dense forests, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from family-friendly paths to demanding climbs. The routes in Vega De Pas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 190 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Vega De Pas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Vega de Pas?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails around Vega de Pas, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored more than 190 of these routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Vega de Pas?
A: The terrain in Vega de Pas is diverse, featuring green meadows, dense forests (including beech and oak), and distinctive karst and limestone landscapes shaped by glacial origins. You'll also encounter river valleys, challenging mountain passes like Alto del Caracol and Puerto de Lunada, and routes that utilize dirt tracks, forest paths, and old mule tracks.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Vega de Pas?
A: Yes, Vega de Pas offers routes suitable for beginners and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999716392/engana-tunnel-and-yera-station-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Engaña Tunnel and Yera Station Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that explores scenic areas. For a more relaxed experience, the Vía Verde del Pas (Pas Greenway) is an excellent option, offering a largely flat, traffic-free path through beautiful natural areas, though it's not explicitly listed in the provided routes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 17 difficult mountain bike routes in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999755834/alto-alcor-and-rubienzo-stream-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Alcor and Rubienzo Stream Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.3 km path featuring challenging gradients and requiring very good fitness. Routes climbing mountain passes like Lunada and Estacas de Trueba also offer significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails integrate natural beauty with cultural heritage. You can encounter green meadows, dense forests, and river valleys. Notable natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Valnera Summit&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular views. You might also pass traditional Pasiego cabins and historic villages.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Vega de Pas?
A: Yes, many routes in Vega de Pas are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999716392/engana-tunnel-and-yera-station-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Engaña Tunnel and Yera Station Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999755834/alto-alcor-and-rubienzo-stream-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Alcor and Rubienzo Stream Loop&lt;/a&gt; are both circular routes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999732034/cascadas-del-rio-troja-circular-desde-san-pedro-del-romeral-por-el-alto-de-la-matanela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascadas del río Troja – circular desde San Pedro del Romeral por el alto de la Matanela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Vega de Pas?
A: Trail durations vary depending on difficulty and distance. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999716392/engana-tunnel-and-yera-station-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Engaña Tunnel and Yera Station Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 26 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999755834/alto-alcor-and-rubienzo-stream-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Alcor and Rubienzo Stream Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes about 2 hours 33 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14992346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Braguía – Sobao Museum loop from Selaya&lt;/a&gt; can take over 3 hours 50 minutes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Vega de Pas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from green meadows to dense forests, and the challenging mountain passes that offer rewarding views. The integration of historical elements like old mule tracks and traditional Pasiego cabins also adds to the unique experience.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the routes?
A: Yes, many routes allow you to connect with the region's rich heritage. You can find ancient mule tracks, small rural chapels, and traditional Pasiego cabins. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3963146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yera ghost station&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3593205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sía Pass&lt;/a&gt; are also notable points of interest that can be incorporated into rides.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Vega de Pas?
A: Vega de Pas, located in Cantabria, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking from spring through autumn. The green meadows and forests are particularly vibrant during these seasons. While specific data isn't provided, the region's diverse landscapes are enjoyable when the weather is mild, avoiding the peak heat of summer or potential winter snow on higher passes.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the Valles Pasiegos region is known for its impressive panoramic views, especially from mountain passes and peaks. Routes that climb passes like Alto del Caracol and Puerto de Lunada will offer significant vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Valnera Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the highest mountain in the eastern Cantabrian range, is highlighted for its spectacular views.
tips: The Aján River waterfalls in Vega de Pas, Cantabria, are a spectacular destination for nature and hiking lovers. The route that follows the course of the Aján River offers a succession of waterfalls, stone bridges and tunnels, surrounded by lush forests and green meadows.

The Engaña Tunnel is a long railway tunnel of almost 17 km. (6,976 meters exactly, measured starting from the South mouth) which is located between the municipalities of Vega de Pas (Cantabria) and Pedrosa de Valdeporres (Burgos), with a width of 8 meters designed to accommodate two roads and a height of 6, 5 m., and takes its name from the nearby Engaña River.

It is part of the Santander-Mediterranean railway project that sought to link the Cantabrian Sea with the Mediterranean and which was never used. A project that was projected to be completed in 4 and a half years was extended for 17 years, being abandoned at the Yera Station, which was the last thing that was done before its abandonment.

Currently it is almost completely collapsed inside (around km 2,800) and since 1999 it has not been possible to drive through it or cross it. At the northern mouth you can see that the water infiltrates from the river seeps through its walls and falls even in a cascade. The construction materials were quite poor and it has degraded very quickly.

The ghost station of Yera is abandoned and in ruins, it is a station that although the works were practically finished, never was inaugurated and was the last to be built of the Santander-Mediterranean railway project. The platforms were finished although the tracks were never laid.
Next to the station there is a small rectangular building that was the old warehouse. And all this set is located on a large concrete platform and a large wall lightened by
32 arches 50 meters high. All of this with spectacular views of the valleys of Pasiegos.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered moderate, requiring good physical fitness and some prior cycling experience. Sections include steep ascents, and you might need to dismount and push your bike at times, especially on stony ground near the Aján River. It's not ideal for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring approximately 14.2 km of unpaved tracks, 3.85 km of asphalt, and 2.75 km of paved tracks. You'll encounter steep climbs and narrow sections. The path alongside the Aján River can be stony and potentially slippery after rain.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the beautiful Aján River Waterfalls, the historic Engaña Tunnel (which you can explore for a few kilometers), and the atmospheric Yera ghost station. You'll also see old stone bridges, smaller tunnels, and remnants of workers' barracks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Vega de Pas?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Vega de Pas. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you should be able to find parking within the village to begin your circular journey. It's always a good idea to arrive early, especially on busy days.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the varied terrain, including stony and potentially slippery sections, ensure your dog is fit for the ride. Always keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, to respect wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to ride this trail, offering pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be warm, and winter may bring colder, wetter conditions, making some sections more challenging due to mud or slippery stones.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to mountain bike in Valles Pasiegos?
faqs.answer: Currently, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike this route in the Valles Pasiegos region. However, always respect local regulations and private property, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: How much of the Engaña Tunnel can I explore?
faqs.answer: The Engaña Tunnel has been impassable since 1999 due to an internal collapse about 2.8 kilometers from the entrance. However, you can still explore a few kilometers into the tunnel from the Cantabrian side (Vega de Pas) to witness its abandoned infrastructure.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. Notably, it covers about 33% of the [CIMA CA05] Estacas de Trueba trail and 11% of the Ferrocarril Santander-Mediterráneo trail, among others like Túnel de El Majoral and Túnel de La Engaña.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay near the trailhead in Vega de Pas?
faqs.answer: Vega de Pas is a charming village and offers options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation. You'll find places to refuel and rest before or after your ride, providing a convenient base for exploring the area.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain biking trip?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's essential to bring appropriate mountain biking gear, including a helmet, gloves, and suitable clothing. Carry enough water, snacks, a basic repair kit, and a headlamp if you plan to explore the Engaña Tunnel.
